title: NOC 41400 – Natural and Applied Science Policy Researchers, Consultants and Program Officers | GTR Immigration
description: Natural and Applied Science Policy Researchers, Consultants and Program Officers under NOC 41400 are professionals who develop, evaluate, and implement science-based policies and programs across Canada. Classified as TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role supports federal, provincial, and municipal governments, as well as private and non-profit sectors in areas such as environmental protection, energy, transportation, occupational health, emergency preparedness, and technology transfer. These professionals bridge the gap between scientific research and public policy, ensuring evidence-based decision-making and sustainable development.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group414 – Professional occupations in government servicesMinor Group4140 – Policy and program researchers, consultants and officersNOC Code41400

common_job_titles: Natural and applied sciences program officer
duties: Professionals in this group may specialize in different fields:
Conduct research on workplace design and human performance
Participate in emergency planning and safety training
Advise clients on patent law and intellectual property
Facilitate collaboration between research institutions and industry
Develop and administer programs related to:TransportationNatural resourcesEnergy technologiesAgricultureEnvironmental protectionLand use and recycling
Promote public awareness on sustainability issues
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree or college diploma in related scientific or technical discipline
Master’s degree may be required for advanced roles
Certification by Canadian Registration Board of Occupational Hygienists
Patent agent examination and registration with Commissioner of Patents
LEED certification (for green building and sustainability roles)
Foreign-trained professionals may require credential assessment for immigration and professional certification purposes.
salary_prospects: Natural and Applied Science Policy Researchers and Program Officers typically earn between:
$51,300 – $115,200 per year, depending on specialization, province, and seniority.
Senior consultants and policy directors may earn significantly higher compensation.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 41400 is positive due to:
Demand for sustainability and environmental specialists continues to grow nationwide.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 41400 are employed in:
Emergency preparedness and disaster management planning
Demand is particularly strong in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Quebec, and Northern territories.
Career advancement into senior policy roles
Opportunities in environmental and sustainability sectors
High demand in science-based program development
Professionals with experience in environmental policy, occupational safety, patent law, and emergency management are particularly valued.
pathways: Professional certifications may be required depending on specialization.
